records.ety: [OE. voute, OF. voute, volte, F. vote, LL. volta, for voluta, volutio, fr. L. volvere, volutum, to roll, to turn about. See Voluble, and cf. Vault a leap, Volt a turn, Volute.]

defs: An arched structure of masonry, forming a ceiling or canopy.
quotes: The long-drawn aisle and fretted vault.

defs: An arched apartment; especially, a subterranean room, used for storing articles, for a prison, for interment, or the like; a cell; a cellar.
quotes: To banish rats that haunt our vault.

defs: The canopy of heaven; the sky.
The bound or leap of a horse; a curvet.
A leap by aid of the hands, or of a pole, springboard, or the like.
notes: ☞ The l in this word was formerly often suppressed in pronunciation.

records.ety: [OE. vouten, OF. volter, vouter, F. voter. See Vault an arch.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Vaulted; p. pr. & vb. n. Vaulting.]

defs: To form with a vault, or to cover with a vault; to give the shape of an arch to; to arch; as, to vault a roof; to vault a passage to a court.
quotes: The shady arch that vaulted the broad green alley.

defs: To leap over; esp., to leap over by aid of the hands or a pole; as, to vault a fence.
quotes: I will vault credit, and affect high pleasures.

records.ety: [Cf. OF. volter, F. voltiger, It. voltare to turn. See Vault, n., 4.]

defs: To leap; to bound; to jump; to spring.
quotes: Leaning on his lance, he vaulted on a tree.
Lucan vaulted upon Pegasus with all the heat and intrepidity of youth.

defs: To exhibit feats of tumbling or leaping; to tumble.
